steps.title: Spot the core trade-off
steps.content: The passage directly contrasts historians' “cold and naked” work with the “mimic charm” of landscape paintings, then reveals why: historians are bound by “literal truth” and “exactness,” methods that conflict with charm and engagement.

steps.title: Apply the constraint to the choices
steps.content: Choice B correctly identifies the tension: when historians prioritize “literal truth” and “biographical exactness,” their work becomes accurate but lacks the engagement of more creative presentations. The other choices either contradict the passage's emphasis on truth or misread the analogy. That matches choice B — B is correct.
